ac:rich-text-body.p: The SKA Set Up Validator checks your observation parameters for feasibility before proposal submission. It validates telescope configuration, frequency settings, bandwidth, and pointing constraints against the capabilities of SKA-Mid and SKA-Low in the requested Array Assembly stage.

p: The Set Up Validator is an automated quality-assurance tool that:

ul.li: Checks Parameter Validity: Ensures frequencies, bandwidths, and integration times are within permitted ranges
Verifies Array Compatibility: Confirms that your requested configuration is available in the selected Array Assembly stage
Validates Pointing Constraints: Checks that target coordinates and elevation limits are physically feasible
Identifies Conflicts: Warns of incompatible parameter combinations (e.g., requested frequency outside receiver passband)
Suggests Corrections: Provides guidance on fixing invalid configurations

p: By catching errors early, the validator saves time during proposal review and reduces the likelihood of unfeasible observations being scheduled.

ul.li: Telescope selection (SKA-Mid or SKA-Low)
Array Assembly stage (AA1, AA2, AA*, AA4)
Subarray assignment (if applicable)
Number of available dishes/stations in the requested stage
Centre frequency within receiver passband
Requested bandwidth does not exceed receiver bandwidth
Spectral-line zoom sub-band is within the full receiver band
No overlap with protected frequency allocations or RFI avoidance bands
Target declination is observable from array latitude
Specified elevation limit is greater than minimum tracking elevation
Right Ascension and Declination are valid (0–24h RA, ±90° Dec)
Transit altitude at culmination is above elevation limit
Requested spectral resolution matches correlator capabilities
Polyphase filterbank configuration is feasible
Data rate does not exceed storage pipeline capacity

h2: Integration with the Proposal Handling Tool (PHT)
p: The Set Up Validator is tightly integrated with the SKA Proposal Handling Tool (PHT):

ul.li: Real-Time Validation: As you configure observations in PHT, the validator runs automatically and highlights issues in real time
Inline Error Reporting: Invalid fields show warnings with explanations and suggested corrections
Submission Blocks: PHT will not permit submission of proposals containing unresolved validation errors
Expert Override: For edge cases or special circumstances, proposers can request validation waivers through the PHT interface (subject to Observatory approval)

h3: Step 1: Configure Your Observation in PHT
p: Enter your observation parameters in the PHT observation editor: telescope, array stage, frequency, bandwidth, pointing centre, etc.
The validator automatically checks your configuration and displays results:

ul.li: GreenValid — Configuration is feasible and will proceed to review
YellowWarning — Non-ideal but acceptable (e.g., high elevation limit reducing observing time)
RedError — Configuration is not feasible and must be corrected

p: For each error or warning, the validator provides guidance:

ul.li: Problem Description: What is invalid or suboptimal
Reason: Why it fails (e.g., "frequency 500 MHz is below SKA-Low minimum of 50 MHz")
Suggested Fix: Recommended adjustment (e.g., "increase frequency to ≥ 50 MHz")
Alternative Options: Feasible configurations that achieve similar science goals

p: After making corrections, the validator re-checks automatically. Once all errors are resolved, you can submit your proposal.

ac:rich-text-body.p: Error: "Frequency 10.5 GHz is above SKA-Mid maximum of 14 GHz."
Solution: Reduce frequency to ≤ 14 GHz, or select SKA-Low (if science permits). For multi-band campaigns, split observations across compatible frequency ranges.
Error: "AA1 stage has only 56 dishes; your observation requires 100 dishes."
Solution: Select a later Array Assembly stage (AA2, AA*, or AA4), or adjust science requirements. You can schedule observations across multiple deployment phases with different array configurations.
Error: "Target at δ = +70° reaches only 45° above horizon; your minimum elevation is 50°."

Solution: Lower the elevation limit (accept poorer sensitivity and higher system temperature), or select a different target. High-declination sources are better observed with SKA-Low at low frequencies.
Error: "Requested data rate 400 Gbps exceeds pipeline capacity of 300 Gbps in AA1."
Solution: Reduce number of subarrays, use coarser spectral channels, or split observation across multiple epochs. Contact the Observatory for custom data rate discussions.

ul.li: Frequency Range: 250 MHz – 14 GHz
Array Stages: AA1 (56 dishes), AA2 (133 dishes), AA* (133 dishes with MID2 hardware), AA4 (197 dishes)
Maximum Bandwidth: Limited by receiver passband (typically 2–8 GHz at high frequencies)
Subarrays: Up to 4 simultaneous subarrays in AA4; fewer in earlier stages
Frequency Range: 50 – 350 MHz
Array Stages: AA1 (~50,000 antennas), AA2 (~100,000 antennas), AA* (~130,000 antennas), AA4 (~130,000+ antennas with band 1/2 upgrade)
Maximum Bandwidth: Full receiver bandwidth (300 MHz typical)
Subarrays: Limited subarray capability; most observations use full array or defined core/extended regions
Run validation frequently as you edit your observation to catch errors early
Use the Sensitivity Calculator alongside the Validator to ensure your parameters are both feasible and sufficient for your science goals
If you receive a warning, read it carefully—it may indicate suboptimal choices that could affect your results
For complex multi-epoch or multi-configuration campaigns, validate each observation separately

ac:rich-text-body.p: If the validator reports an error you believe is incorrect, or if you need a waiver for a special configuration, contact the SKA Observatory observing support team via the PHT help system.
